items.title: Nearly 300 homes damaged or destroyed by wildfires in Oklahoma, Gov. Stitt says
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>A massive storm system moving across the United States unleashed winds that triggered deadly dust storms and fanned wildfires. Nearly 300 homes have been damaged or destroyed in Oklahoma due to fire. 


Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Russian military shows footage of key town retaken from Ukraine in Kursk region
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Sudzha, with a reported population of about 5,000 people before the offensive, was the biggest town seized by Kyiv's forces last year as part of their surprise cross-border offensive. The Russian army, backed by North Korean troops, are now close to completely driving Ukrainian forces from their foothold in the Kursk region in what would be a major setback for Kyiv. 


Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Scientists are studying how ultraprocessed foods affect our health
items.author: Associated Press
items.content: <article>Ultraprocessed foods make up over 70% of the U.S. food supply, but how do they really affect our health? This NIH study is trying to find out. #shorts #health #ultraprocessedfoods</article>

items.title: Ukrainian residents left reeling after shelling in Kherson
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Residential buildings and civil infrastructure have been destroyed in Kherson in southern Ukraine, after the city was hit by Russian shelling late on Thursday, Ukrainian authorities said. A man was killed and three others were injured when a bomb hit Kherson's central district. 



Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Elevated road under construction in Bangkok collapses, killing at least 5 people
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The collapse, which happened in the early morning in southwestern Bangkok, also injured 24 other people at the construction site, said Transport Minister Suriya Jungrungreangkit.


Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

items.title: 'How can we survive without support?': US aid cuts hit Ethiopia region
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Aid agencies distributing U.S. food aid in Ethiopia's war-affected Tigray region say they have had to stop feeding millions of people because of the Trump administration's restrictions on foreign aid. Read more: https://bit.ly/41PceAl 

#ethiopia #trump</article>

items.title: JD Vance booed by crowd at Kennedy Center concert
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>U.S. Vice President JD Vance and Second Lady Usha Vance were booed by a crowd as they attended a concert in Washington, D.C. They were jeered by those who watched the National Symphony Orchestra at the Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts on Thursday.

#jdvance 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Mexico's president reacts to possible killing site discovered in Jalisco
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Mexico's President Claudia Sheinbaum said on Friday that her government has no political interest in the case of the discovery of a possible mass killing site in Jalisco state, but that of seeking the truth on what happened. (AP Video by Diana de la Mora). Read more: https://bit.ly/41SnITF 

#mexico #news #jalisco 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

items.title: SpaceX launches crew to space station to replace NASA's stuck astronauts
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The replacements for NASA’s two stuck astronauts launched to the International Space Station on Friday night, paving the way for the pair’s return after nine long months.

#spacex #nasa #space 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Senate passes six-month funding bill hours before shutdown deadline
items.author: Associated Press
items.content: <article>The Senate has passed a six-month spending bill hours before a government shutdown, overcoming sharp Democratic opposition to the measure. The bill now goes to President Donald Trump to be signed into law. #governmentshutdown #Senate #Trump</article>

items.title: LIVE: Launch of NASA’s SpaceX Crew-10 mission
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Watch live launch coverage of NASA’s SpaceX Crew-10 mission to the International Space Station.

A launch pad problem prompted SpaceX to delay the flight on Wednesday to replace NASA’s two stuck astronauts. The new crew needs to get to the International Space Station before Butch Wilmore and Suni Williams can head home after nine months in orbit.

Credit: NASA/SpaceX
#nasa #live #spacex</article>

items.title: Zelenskyy reaffirms Ukraine is ready to accept US ceasefire proposal with Russia
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy reaffirmed on Friday that Ukraine is ready to accept a U.S. "offer" of a 30-day ceasefire with Russia.

#ukraine #russia #zelenskyy

items.title: LIVE: Senate passes six-month funding bill
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The Senate passed a six-month spending bill on Friday, hours before a government shutdown, overcoming sharp Democratic opposition to the measure and sending it to President Donald Trump to be signed into law.

The vote was 54-46. Live updates: https://bit.ly/3FxNR2T

#live #senate #news</article>

items.title: Trump pledges to ‘expose’ his enemies in speech at Justice Department
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>President Donald Trump used a speech at the Justice Department to air a litany of grievances about the criminal investigations he faced, decrying his adversaries in often profane terms and casting himself as a victim of unfair and biased prosecutions. Read more: https://bit.ly/4iOzzJj 

#trump #news #donaldtrump 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Attorneys for Mahmoud Khalil release video of his arrest
items.author: Associated Press
items.content: <article>Attorneys for Mahmoud Khalil shared video of his arrest inside his university-owned residence last weekend. The clip shows immigration agents confronting Khalil in the building’s lobby. #MahmoudKhalil #arrest #caughtoncamera #Columbia #ColumbiaUniversity</article>
